Off Calendar Jan-18-2022 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Robert Kern appeared by telephone for and with Jose Abrego by zoom and Rosemary Abrego-Holmes by zoom.

The Court noted that no one is present to object to the petition. Mr. Kern stated that no one has indicated that there are any objections, that this petition is essentially exactly the same as the prior petition, except that they deleted the alternative request and that he thinks notice to the parties should be considered satisfactory. Mr. Kern also stated that a waiver of bond signed by the Grandson Antonio Sandoval was filed on Friday. The Court noted that the change was substantial, and stated that it will waive defect regarding notice and waive publication of this petition. The Court also stated that the evidence presented is enough to rebut the presumption of undue influence. The Court granted the Second Amended Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated September 12, 2008 is admitted to probate. Rosemary Abrego-Holmes and Jose Abrego are appointed as the Administrators of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for November 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Nov-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Kern appeared by zoom for Rosemary Abrego Homes and Jose Abrego.

The Court informed Mr. Kern that it does not take petitions that request alternative relief for appointment. Discussion was held regarding Probate Code 6112 and the presumption of fraud affecting the burden of proof. Mr. Kern referred to the attachments to the several proofs of subscribing witness that were provided on June 2nd and stated that he has the authority to make verifications on behalf of his Clients regarding facts relating to the Decedent on the basis of information and belief without having signatures. Mr. Kern also stated that the will was dictated by the Decedent and written down by Rosemary in the Decedent's presence and in the presence of other witnesses, that he believes the need to produce evidence has been taken care of and that they have a declaration as a proof of service. In addition, Mr. Kern stated that it is a valid will and that he thinks the will should be admitted to probate. The Court informed Mr. Kern that the will will not be admitted under the probate code, that the presumption of undue influence affecting the burden of proof will not be carried by a self-serving declaration, and that it will not appoint someone who is under a legal assumption to have committed fraud and have exercised undue influence. The Court also suggested amending the petition to choose one path with these parties, or choose another path with a third party representative who is untainted by the circumstances of this will. The Amended Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to November 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
